title: Motor Error
shortDescription: The fan motor has failed to start or is running at an incorrect speed.
fullDescription: The E1 error code on Xiaomi air purifiers indicates the fan motor is not operating correctly. The motor may fail to start, run at the wrong speed, or stop unexpectedly. The control board monitors motor feedback and triggers E1 when expected performance is not met.

causes.cause: Dust and debris clogging the fan assembly
Motor control circuit failure on the main board

symptoms: E1 displayed on the unit or in the Mi Home app
Fan does not spin
Fan spins very slowly or intermittently
Humming noise from the motor area
Unit powers on but provides no airflow

fixSteps.title: Clean the fan assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the unit. Remove the filter(s) and any accessible covers. Use compressed air or a soft brush to clean dust from the fan blades and motor housing. Heavy dust buildup can prevent the motor from spinning.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the unit
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the air purifier for 5 minutes, then plug it back in and attempt to start it. A temporary motor glitch may clear with a reset.

fixSteps.title: Check motor connections
fixSteps.instruction: If comfortable, open the unit housing and verify the motor wire connector is secure on the main board.

fixSteps.title: Replace the motor
fixSteps.instruction: If the motor is confirmed defective, order a replacement and swap it out. This typically requires disassembling the unit.

whenToCallPro: If motor replacement is needed and you are not comfortable disassembling the unit.
videoSearchQuery: Xiaomi air purifier E1 error motor fix

faq.q: Can dust cause E1?
faq.a: Yes. Heavy dust on the fan blades or motor can prevent proper rotation. Regular cleaning prevents this.

faq.q: Will the Mi Home app show E1?
faq.a: Yes. The app will display the error code and may provide basic troubleshooting steps.

faq.q: How long do Xiaomi air purifier motors last?
faq.a: Typically 3 to 5 years with regular use and proper filter maintenance.

title: Filter Not Installed or Not Detected
shortDescription: The air purifier cannot detect a filter installed in the unit.
fullDescription: The E2 error code indicates the air purifier does not detect a filter in position. The filter presence sensor or the filter itself may not be making proper contact. Some models use RFID chips on the filter for detection.

causes.cause: Filter not installed or not fully seated
Dirty or damaged filter contacts or RFID tag
Third-party filter without compatible RFID chip

symptoms: E2 displayed on screen or in Mi Home app
Unit will not start
Filter replacement reminder appears despite new filter
Unit beeps when powered on

fixSteps.title: Reinstall the filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and reinstall the filter, ensuring it is fully seated and locked into position. Listen for a click confirming proper engagement.

fixSteps.title: Clean filter contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Wipe the filter RFID chip or contact area with a dry cloth. Also clean the corresponding sensor area inside the unit.

fixSteps.title: Try an official filter
fixSteps.instruction: If using a third-party filter, try an official Xiaomi filter. Third-party filters may not have compatible RFID chips.

fixSteps.title: Reset filter counter via Mi Home app
fixSteps.instruction: Open the Mi Home app, navigate to the purifier settings, and reset the filter life counter. Some E2 errors occur when the counter reaches zero.

whenToCallPro: Only if the filter sensor itself is defective, which is rare.
videoSearchQuery: Xiaomi air purifier E2 error filter not detected fix

faq.q: Can I use third-party filters?
faq.a: Some third-party filters work, but those without an RFID chip may trigger E2. Look for filters specifically labeled as Xiaomi-compatible.

faq.q: How do I reset the filter counter?
faq.a: In the Mi Home app, go to the purifier's device page, tap the filter section, and select reset.

faq.q: Does E2 mean my filter is expired?
faq.a: Not necessarily. E2 means the filter is not detected. It could be improperly seated or the counter may need resetting.

title: Laser Particle Sensor Error
shortDescription: The laser particle sensor is malfunctioning or blocked.
fullDescription: The E3 error code indicates the PM2.5 laser particle sensor is not providing reliable readings. The sensor uses a laser to count particles in the air and may be blocked by dust, moisture, or have failed internally.

causes.cause: Dust buildup on the sensor lens or intake
Moisture or condensation inside the sensor

symptoms: E3 displayed on screen
Air quality reading shows dashes or zero
AQI display stuck at one value
Auto mode does not adjust fan speed properly
Unit runs but does not respond to air quality changes

fixSteps.title: Clean the particle sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the sensor on the side or rear of the unit. Use a cotton swab or compressed air to gently clean the sensor intake opening and any visible lens area. Some models have a removable sensor that can be cleaned more thoroughly.

fixSteps.title: Allow moisture to dry
fixSteps.instruction: If the unit was in a humid environment or near steam, move it to a dry area and allow 24 hours for moisture to evaporate from the sensor.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor connection
fixSteps.instruction: If comfortable, open the unit and verify the sensor cable is securely connected to the main board.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If cleaning does not resolve E3, the sensor module may need replacement. Order the correct sensor for your model.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor module needs replacement and you cannot source the part.
videoSearchQuery: Xiaomi air purifier E3 error particle sensor fix

faq.q: Can I use the purifier without the sensor?
faq.a: The purifier will run in manual mode but auto mode will not function correctly without the particle sensor.

faq.q: How often should I clean the sensor?
faq.a: Clean the sensor opening every 2 to 3 months, or monthly in dusty environments.

faq.q: Does cooking steam affect the sensor?
faq.a: Yes. Steam and cooking aerosols can coat the sensor lens or cause temporary E3. Move the purifier away from kitchens.

title: Temperature/Humidity Sensor Error
shortDescription: The temperature and humidity sensor has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: The E4 error code indicates the onboard temperature and humidity sensor is returning abnormal readings. This sensor helps optimize purifier operation and provides environmental data to the Mi Home app.

causes.cause: Sensor exposed to extreme temperature or moisture
Loose sensor connection on the PCB

symptoms: E4 displayed on screen or app
Incorrect temperature or humidity readings
Auto mode behaves erratically
Dashes shown for temp/humidity values
Unit functions but environmental data is wrong

fixSteps.title: Move the unit to a moderate environment
fixSteps.instruction: Extreme cold, heat, or humidity can cause temporary sensor faults. Move the purifier to a room with 15 to 35 degree Celsius temperature and moderate humidity. Wait 30 minutes and check if E4 clears.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the unit
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes and restart.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor connection
fixSteps.instruction: Open the unit housing and verify the sensor module is connected to the main board.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor module
fixSteps.instruction: If E4 persists, order and install a replacement temperature/humidity sensor module.

whenToCallPro: Only if you cannot source or install the replacement sensor.
videoSearchQuery: Xiaomi air purifier E4 error temperature humidity sensor fix

faq.q: Does E4 prevent the purifier from working?
faq.a: The purifier will still run in manual mode. Auto mode may be unreliable without accurate sensor data.

faq.q: Can a bathroom or kitchen cause E4?
faq.a: Yes. High humidity environments can overwhelm or damage the sensor. Air purifiers should not be placed in wet locations.

faq.q: Is the sensor replaceable?
faq.a: Yes. The sensor module is typically a small component connected to the main board and can be replaced.

title: Wi-Fi Module Error
shortDescription: The Wi-Fi communication module has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: The E5 error code indicates the Wi-Fi module that connects the purifier to the Mi Home app and network has failed or lost its configuration. The purifier will still operate locally but cannot be controlled remotely.

causes.cause: Main board communication failure with Wi-Fi module

symptoms: E5 displayed on unit
Cannot connect to Mi Home app
Wi-Fi indicator light off or blinking rapidly
Purifier works manually but not via app
Network setup fails repeatedly

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the purifier
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes. Plug back in. The Wi-Fi module may recover after a full restart.

fixSteps.title: Reset Wi-Fi settings
fixSteps.instruction: Press and hold the Wi-Fi reset button (small pinhole on the back or bottom) for 10 seconds until the indicator light blinks. Reconfigure in the Mi Home app.

fixSteps.title: Check router compatibility
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure your router broadcasts a 2.4 GHz network. Xiaomi purifiers typically do not support 5 GHz Wi-Fi. Check that the router is within range.

fixSteps.title: Factory reset the purifier
fixSteps.instruction: Consult the manual for the factory reset procedure. This clears all settings and allows a fresh Wi-Fi setup.

whenToCallPro: If the Wi-Fi module hardware has failed and manual/local operation is insufficient.
videoSearchQuery: Xiaomi air purifier E5 error WiFi fix

faq.q: Does E5 prevent the purifier from cleaning air?
faq.a: No. The purifier works normally in manual mode. Only app control and remote monitoring are affected.

faq.q: Why does my purifier only connect to 2.4 GHz?
faq.a: Most IoT devices including Xiaomi purifiers use 2.4 GHz for its better range and lower power requirements.

faq.q: Can I use the purifier without Wi-Fi?
faq.a: Yes. All purifiers can be operated using the physical buttons on the unit.

title: Main Board Error
shortDescription: The main control board has detected an internal fault.
fullDescription: The E6 error code indicates the main control board has experienced a critical fault, possibly from a power surge, component failure, or firmware corruption. The purifier may be completely unresponsive.

causes.cause: Power surge damage to board components
Failed capacitor or relay on the board

symptoms: E6 displayed on startup
Unit completely unresponsive
No fan operation or display
Unit worked fine before a power outage
Burning smell from electronics area

fixSteps.title: Full power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes to discharge all capacitors. Plug back in and check.

fixSteps.title: Check power supply
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the wall outlet provides correct voltage and the power cord is not damaged.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the board visually
fixSteps.instruction: If comfortable, open the unit and look for burned components, swollen capacitors, or discoloration on the PCB.

fixSteps.title: Replace the main board
fixSteps.instruction: If the board is damaged, order a replacement for your model. Swap the boards, reconnecting all cables.

whenToCallPro: Board diagnosis and replacement is best handled by a professional unless you have electronics experience.
videoSearchQuery: Xiaomi air purifier E6 error main board fix

faq.q: Is a burning smell dangerous?
faq.a: Yes. Unplug immediately. A burning smell indicates a component has failed and could be a fire risk.

faq.q: Is it worth replacing the board?
faq.a: If the board costs $25-60 and the purifier is otherwise in good condition, replacement is usually worthwhile.

faq.q: Can a power surge cause E6?
faq.a: Yes. Use a surge protector for all smart appliances.

title: Fan Speed Feedback Error
shortDescription: The fan speed sensor is not returning expected feedback.
fullDescription: The E7 error code indicates the main board is not receiving correct fan speed feedback from the motor. This prevents the purifier from maintaining the set fan speed and affects auto-mode operation.

causes.cause: Defective fan speed sensor or hall sensor
Motor bearing failure causing speed fluctuations

symptoms: E7 on display or app
Fan speed fluctuates erratically
Purifier cannot maintain set speed
Noise changes during operation
Auto mode does not respond correctly

fixSteps.title: Clean the fan assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug, remove filters, and thoroughly clean the fan blades and motor area with compressed air and a soft brush.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes and restart.

fixSteps.title: Check motor cables
fixSteps.instruction: Open the unit and verify the motor feedback cable is securely connected.

fixSteps.title: Replace motor or sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the speed sensor is faulty, replace the motor assembly which typically includes the sensor.

videoSearchQuery: Xiaomi air purifier E7 error fan speed sensor fix

faq.q: Can dust cause E7?
faq.a: Yes. Heavy dust buildup on the fan can create enough drag to prevent proper speed maintenance.

faq.q: Is the speed sensor separate from the motor?
faq.a: On most Xiaomi models, the sensor is integrated into the motor assembly and they are replaced as a unit.

faq.q: Does E7 affect air purification?
faq.a: Yes. Without proper speed control, the purifier cannot deliver the rated CADR (Clean Air Delivery Rate).

title: Display Module Error
shortDescription: The OLED or LED display module is malfunctioning.
fullDescription: The E8 error code indicates the display panel on the purifier is not communicating properly with the main board. The display may be blank, flickering, or showing incorrect information.
symptoms: E8 reported in Mi Home app
Display is blank or partially lit
Display shows random characters
Brightness does not adjust
Touch controls on display not responsive

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes. A temporary display glitch may clear.

fixSteps.title: Check display cable
fixSteps.instruction: Open the unit and locate the display ribbon cable. Disconnect and firmly reseat it.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the display
fixSteps.instruction: Check for physical damage, cracks, or moisture on the display module.

fixSteps.title: Replace the display module
fixSteps.instruction: If the display is defective, order a replacement and swap the module.

whenToCallPro: Only if the main board display driver is at fault.
videoSearchQuery: Xiaomi air purifier E8 error display fix

faq.q: Can I still use the purifier with E8?
faq.a: Yes. The purifier can be controlled via the Mi Home app even if the physical display is not working.

faq.q: Does the display affect purification?
faq.a: No. The display is for user interface only. Air purification is unaffected.

faq.q: Is the display module expensive?
faq.a: No. Display modules typically cost $10 to $30.
